title: Certificate-Based OPC-UA Logon Failures and Weak Trust Stores

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 1.9.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.9 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.16: Certificate-Based OPC-UA Logon Failures and Weak Trust Stores.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.16' running on edge_hub_opcua or unified sourcetype with security_policy, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-7d
| search status!="Good" OR match(_raw,"(?i)BadCertificate|issuer|untrusted")
| eval server=coalesce(opcua_server_endpoint, dest_host)
| stats count by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type
| sort - count
description: Tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_opcua. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Deploy UA GDS or centralized PKI; (2) map status codes to runbooks; (3) alert on spike per server; (4) rotate OPC-UA app certs on schedule. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_opcua`.
visualization: Table, Time chart, Single value (failed channels). Key fields: `opcua_security_mode`, `opcua_user_token_type`, `server`, `status`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub / unified `index=ot_protocol`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_opcua` or unified sourcetype with `security_policy`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deploy UA GDS or centralized PKI; (2) map status codes to runbooks; (3) alert on spike per server; (4) rotate OPC-UA app certs on schedule.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-7d
| search status!="Good" OR match(_raw,"(?i)BadCertificate|issuer|untrusted")
| eval server=coalesce(opcua_server_endpoint, dest_host)
| stats count by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Certificate-Based OPC-UA Logon Failures and Weak Trust Stores (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.9)** — Tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_opcua` or unified sourcetype with `security_policy`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub / unified `index=ot_protocol`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_opcua. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **server** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Time chart, Single value (failed channels).
grandmaExplanation: We tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
